what_it_does: This file is an IIFE that mounts a per-unit brain UI onto rendered gallery and decision units. It listens for gallery:rendered and decision:rendered custom events, attaches an Ask about this toggle panel to the supplied DOM root, and forwards the operator's question to window.forkBrainCore.talk keyed by the unit's source or decision address. It also listens for gallery:direction events, batches them in a microtask, and forwards the batch to window.forkBrainCore.writeDirections.
observations: The module expects fork-brain-core.js to be loaded first and accesses it through window.forkBrainCore.
bindBrain uses a data-brainBound flag to avoid attaching the affordance twice to the same element.
The panel is toggled by creating/removing a child div with .brain-panel, .brain-reply, .brain-input, and .brain-send classes.
It binds to both gallery:rendered and decision:rendered events, using detail.element/root and detail.source/address/decision.
gallery:direction items are collected into _pending and flushed once via Promise.resolve().then(flush).
A comment explicitly states this file does not consume gallery:verb because App.tsx's dispatcher owns that route.
standouts: The decision:rendered hook extends the same Ask affordance to decision cards, keyed by the decision's canonical address.
The operator-law comment forbids displaying the tool name or raw code:// address to the operator.
The explicit self-correction note explaining why gallery:verb is intentionally not handled here to prevent double writes.
conventions: IIFE wrapper to avoid leaking variables into global scope except the intended window.forkBrainHooks.
Custom event-driven binding rather than direct function calls from DNA.
data-* attribute guard against duplicate DOM attachment.
Global singleton dependency accessed through a helper core().
concerns: No visible error handling if core().talk or core().writeDirections throws or rejects.
Event listeners added to the send button are not explicitly removed when the panel is toggled off, though the element is removed.
The _pending batch array is unbounded until the microtask runs.
It assumes detail.element/root is a real Element and source/address/decision is present; malformed events are silently ignored.
notes: Per-unit brain binder: attaches an Ask about this panel to gallery/decision units and batches direction writebacks through a shared core.
questions: What is the exact contract for forkBrainCore.talk and writeDirections (argument shapes and return types)?
How does DNA style the .brain-ask / .brain-panel classes, and where are those styles defined?
What happens if a bound unit is removed from the DOM while a brain stream is active?
Is there any retry or error surfacing mechanism when writeDirections fails?
What fields does decision:rendered reliably provide, and is d.decision ever different from d.address?
Why is d.source preferred over d.address in gallery:rendered when the comment says source is the canonical code:// address?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is a per-unit mount that wires rendered units to the shared brain core and routes direction writebacks.
apparent_themes: Reuse-don't-parallel: one brain-turn engine in the core, multiple mount files.
Event-driven, race-safe binding after DNA renders.
intention_signals: It is trying to be a thin, reusable DOM-to-core adapter that lets any rendered unit gain a conversational brain affordance and route operator directions back to territory, without reimplementing the brain-turn engine.
novelty: The decision-card extension and the explicit, commented decision to not consume gallery:verb to avoid a double-write with App.tsx's dispatcher.
contribution: A per-unit brain UI binder and a microtask-batched direction forwarder that keeps the brain-turn implementation in one place.
summary_for_embedding: A browser IIFE that attaches an Ask about this brain panel to gallery and decision units via custom events and forwards operator questions and batched direction items to a shared window.forkBrainCore.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as the per-unit mount surface for the fork brain, wiring DNA-rendered units to the centralized brain-turn engine and routing writebacks without duplicating core logic.

symbols.does: Returns the shared window.forkBrainCore singleton, throwing if it is not loaded.
symbols.description: The core() helper centralizes access to the brain-turn engine. It is called lazily inside event handlers and guards against a missing dependency by throwing an explicit error.

symbols.does: Attaches a toggle Ask about this button and panel to a DOM element keyed by an address.
symbols.description: bindBrain creates a .brain-ask button and, on click, appends a .brain-panel containing a reply area, textarea, and send button. It wires the send action to core().talk so the operator's follow-up is scoped to the unit's source or decision address.

symbols.does: Reads the panel's textarea and invokes the brain-turn engine for the bound address.
symbols.description: The local send handler trims the operator's question and, if non-empty, calls core().talk with the reply container, the unit's address, and the question text.

symbols.does: Drains the pending gallery:direction batch and forwards it to the core write-back function.
symbols.description: flush swaps the _pending array and passes its contents to core().writeDirections, implementing the microtask-batched route-back for direction items collected from gallery:direction events.
